deltas.hypothesis: Adding one numeric constant, one numeric indicator, and one aligned wire introduces a single auto-chain compressed-wire chunk with no trailing geometry.
deltas.evidence: Numeric42.vi is the baseline for the 0208 straight-wire sentinel used by ChainAutoPath.

deltas.hypothesis: Changing an integer numeric constant to floating-point changes VCTP/type metadata while preserving the simple auto-chain topology.
deltas.evidence: Both fixtures remain simple single-wire networks; descriptor-level interpretation still needs VCTP Inner decoding.

deltas.hypothesis: Boolean terminals use the same straight auto-chain wire family as numeric terminals, while widget class mapping changes from numeric to boolean.
deltas.evidence: BoolToLED.vi provides a non-numeric straight-wire control/indicator pair.

deltas.hypothesis: Moving the sink horizontally changes terminal geometry but does not require a new source-side chain payload value; the renderer stretches the post-elbow segment from terminal bounds.
deltas.evidence: Numeric42Far.vi is used as the far-spacing control for ChainAutoPath rendering.

deltas.hypothesis: Moving the sink 8 px down changes the auto-chain y-step magnitude to 8 while preserving source anchor semantics.
deltas.evidence: Wire tests compare the decoded ChainAutoPath y-step against the controlled 8 px fixture.

deltas.hypothesis: Increasing the vertical displacement from 8 px to 16 px doubles the y-step magnitude.
deltas.evidence: The pair separates magnitude from sign and horizontal routing effects.

deltas.hypothesis: The auto-chain geometry payload is a sequence of raw single bytes, not an LEB128 varint stream. A 150 px y-step is the single byte 0x96; LEB128 would emit two bytes (96 01) and lengthen the chunk. Crossing the 127 boundary is what distinguishes the two encodings — every prior controlled probe stayed under 128, so they were indistinguishable.
deltas.evidence: Numeric42_16px_down.vi = 04 08 00 00 41 10 (yStep 0x10=16); Numeric42_150px_down.vi = 04 08 00 00 41 96 (yStep 0x96=150 as ONE byte). The old LEB128 parser mis-read 0x96 as a truncated continuation and dropped it (chain-geometry [0 0 65]); raw-byte parsing yields [0 0 65 150]. This also removed the over-fitted {16,65} anchor whitelist, unlocking ~8 real single-elbow chunks (write-ini.vi, ndjson-parser.vi, is-string.vi) whose anchors/y-steps exceed 127.

deltas.hypothesis: Auto-chain geometry magnitudes < 255 are single raw bytes (150 = 0x96); magnitudes >= 255 use a 0xff escape introducing a 2-byte big-endian value. Numeric42FarFar.vi (sink ~400 px down, > 400 px right) is 04 08 00 00 e2 ff 01 90 -> anchorX=0xe2=226, yStep=0xff 01 90=0x0190=400.
deltas.evidence: yStep decodes to EXACTLY 400 for a 400 px move, confirming 1:1 pixel scaling for escaped (>= 255) magnitudes. Big-endian is forced: little-endian gives 0x9001. The same escape resolves the three previously-undecodable real chunks (is-float.vi 2d ff 01 28 -> [45,296]; is-int.vi ff 01 85 ff 03 3e -> [389,830]; is-float.vi ff 03 05 ff 02 35 -> [773,565]). Separately, anchorX=226 despite a >400 px horizontal offset (and 470 in an earlier save at a smaller x) confirms anchorX is the edit-history-dependent elbow offset, not the sink's x distance, which lives in the terminal bounds.

deltas.hypothesis: Moving the sink above the source changes the direction flag while preserving y-step magnitude.
deltas.evidence: The up/down fixture pair ground-truths ChainAutoPath sign handling.

deltas.hypothesis: Two disconnected const-to-indicator pairs emit two independent compressed-wire chunks.
deltas.evidence: CountWireMix observes two auto-chain networks in the two-network fixture.

deltas.hypothesis: Moving each branch endpoint perturbs the corresponding trailing endpoint record while earlier structural records remain topology data.
deltas.evidence: TreeEndpointPair is grounded by this geometry-varied pair.

deltas.hypothesis: byte0=7 covers two visually distinct fan-outs that share a header: a pure vertical Y-stack (reference-find-by-id.vi, all small (V, H) endpoints) and a T-fork. The block-diagram image of Numeric42ThreeIndicatorsTfork.vi shows the latter — Numeric 3 sits far right at mid-height and decodes as a genuine endpoint {V:66, H:196}, while Numeric 2 / Numeric tap up and down off the junction. The last waypoint-count-minus-4 records track per-branch geometry, but only the straight-through leg's two bytes are a true (V, H) coordinate.
deltas.evidence: Moving the bottom tap 8 px DOWN changes exactly one trailing record (44 2d -> 44 35, +8), but in the byte TreeEndpoints labels H rather than V — so a tap record's second byte is a branch offset, not a horizontal coordinate. Full per-axis decoding of tap records needs a controlled horizontal-move fixture (move Numeric 3 right by a known px to confirm H=196 scales 1:1).

deltas.hypothesis: Comb payloads carry junction/span records around the moved branch rather than a simple trailing endpoint list.
deltas.evidence: The middle-branch move changes adjacent records in opposite directions, so byte0 >= 8 shapes stay raw until more comb variations are authored.

deltas.hypothesis: Four-output networks produce larger topology-dependent tree payloads that need additional fixture variation before endpoint extraction is safe.
deltas.evidence: The larger tree fixture is intentionally not decoded by TreeEndpoints.

deltas.hypothesis: reference-find-by-id.vi is the clean pure-vertical-Y-stack case of byte0=7 (every trailing record a small (V, H) endpoint), distinct from the controlled T-fork; it is the independent real-corpus sanity check that the trailing-record rule appears outside authored fixtures.
deltas.evidence: reference-find-by-id.vi carries a matching byte0=7 shape with no far-right outlier record, used as an independent sanity check.

deltas.hypothesis: Placing the indicator left of the source produces a 6-waypoint chain payload (06 08 00 00 00 00 10 0f 34 23) instead of the 4-waypoint single-elbow form (04 08 00 00 41 08). Ground truth for the byte0=6 multi-elbow shape that ChainAutoPath rejects (>4 varints).
deltas.evidence: Real on-disk bytes confirmed by pkg/lvvi decode. indicator_left is a near-aligned variant (prefix 10 0f, p1=00) distinct from the calibrated left_auto family below.

deltas.hypothesis: For the byte0=6 leftward auto route, moving the indicator from 8px to 16px above the source changes only the trailing byte p7 (06 08 00 01 01 00 10 10 9c 18 vs 9c 20), i.e. p7 = 16 + vertical pixels at 1 unit/pixel.
deltas.evidence: Wire chunks identical except p7 (0x18 -> 0x20, +8 for +8px); decoded by Wire.LeftwardChainPath and pinned in pkg/lvvi ground-truth tests.

deltas.hypothesis: Flipping the indicator from 8px above to 8px below the source (same X) changes only p0/p2 (up=(00,01) -> down=(01,00)) while the magnitude p7 and horizontal p6 stay fixed, isolating vertical direction from magnitude and horizontal position.

Moving the indicator 8px right (closer to the source) changes only p6 (06 08 01 01 00 00 10 10 9c 18 vs 94 18), i.e. p6 = horizontal seed at 1 unit/pixel, decreasing as the sink approaches the source. Horizontal IS stored in the byte0=6 chunk (unlike the byte0=4 single-step wires, where x comes from terminal bounds).
deltas.evidence: Wire chunks identical except p6 (0x9c -> 0x94, -8 for +8px right); decoded by Wire.LeftwardChainPath.

deltas.hypothesis: Manual elbow edits made through the LabVIEW editor's normal drag handles get cleaned up by the auto-router when the resulting path is still expressible as a single L-shape; the saved payload retains byte1=0x08 (auto) rather than promoting to byte1=0x04 (manual). Confirms Numeric42Bend.vi remains the only manual-chain ground truth in the corpus.
deltas.evidence: Saved content is 04 08 00 00 fe 70 — same 4-waypoint single-elbow shape as Numeric42_8px_down.vi (04 08 00 00 41 08); only the y-step magnitude varint differs.

deltas.hypothesis: Two VIs with identical terminal positions but different edit history carry different wire chunks. Stretching a wire (moving the sink after drawing) freezes the elbow offset (04 08 00 00 41 08, sourceAnchorX=65); deleting and reconnecting it recomputes the elbow at the canonical short distance (04 08 00 00 10 08, sourceAnchorX=16). sourceAnchorX is therefore edit-history-dependent, not a glyph constant; only yStep reflects real geometry.
deltas.evidence: Same indicator position in both; wire chunk differs only at payload[2] (0x41=65 stretched vs 0x10=16 reconnected). Confirmed via lvrsrc wires and Wire.ChainAutoPath. Second, cleaner witness with no horizontal confound: Numeric42_8px_down.vi (stretched, 04 08 00 00 41 08) vs Numeric42_8px_down_auto-connect.vi (deleted wire, moved indicator 8px down, reconnected -> fresh route 04 08 00 00 10 08) — identical 8px-down geometry, anchorX 65 vs 16.

deltas.hypothesis: A block-diagram numeric-constant literal lives in OF__ConstValue (tag 589) as a fixed-width big-endian int32 written verbatim, NOT the byte-stream-with-0xff-escape used by the compressed-wire geometry. Crossing the single-byte boundary (255->256) does not change the field width or insert an escape byte.
deltas.evidence: OF__ConstValue leaf: 255 = 00 00 00 ff, 256 = 00 00 01 00 (HeapScalarForTag Signed=255/256). Pure positional big-endian, no 0xff prefix, width stays 4. Decoded via pkg/lvvi.HeapConstValue; pinned by TestHeapConstValueGroundTruth.

deltas.hypothesis: OF__ConstValue stays a fixed 4-byte big-endian int32 across the two-byte boundary; the literal width tracks the constant data type (I32 here), independent of magnitude.
deltas.evidence: OF__ConstValue leaf: 65535 = 00 00 ff ff, 65536 = 00 01 00 00 (Signed=65535/65536). No varint growth, no escape; confirms type-width fixed BE encoding. Pinned by TestHeapConstValueGroundTruth.

deltas.hypothesis: Inserting a left-to-right primitive (Increment) keeps both terminals side-entered, so each wire stays a 2-corner byte0=04 elbow. An odd-corner byte0=03 route (1 corner) is unreachable when source exits right and sink enters left, since joining two parallel horizontal stubs needs an even corner count.
deltas.evidence: Numeric42_increment.vi: constant->increment input = 04 08 01 00 0c 08 (yStep -8, anchorX 12); increment output->indicator = 04 08 00 00 10 08 (yStep 8, anchorX 16). Both byte0=04, no 03. Establishes waypoints = corners + 2.

deltas.hypothesis: Even a top-entered terminal (For-Loop count N) keeps a short horizontal stub, so the route stays a 2-corner byte0=04 elbow. byte0=03 (single-corner L) is an emergent multi-node artifact, not coercible from a single source-to-sink constant layout; the 10 real-world byte0=03 chunks all come from function-node VIs.
deltas.evidence: Numeric42_N_terminal.vi: 04 08 01 00 0e 3e (yStep -62) and 04 08 00 00 10 5e (yStep 94). Both byte0=04 despite the top-entered N terminal. No Numeric42* probe in any orientation (down/up/left/right/N/case) yields a 03; leftward routing is byte0=06.

deltas.hypothesis: The OF__ConstValue literal width tracks the constant data type: an I32 constant is a 4-byte big-endian int32, a DBL constant is an 8-byte big-endian IEEE-754 double, both written verbatim with no escape.
deltas.evidence: Numeric42.vi OF__ConstValue = 00 00 00 2a (4 bytes, I32 42); Numeric9876Dot5432.vi OF__ConstValue = 40 c3 4a 45 87 93 dd 98 (8 bytes, DBL 9876.5432 via big-endian IEEE-754). Pinned by TestHeapConstValueGroundTruth and TestHeapConstValueDBLGroundTruth.

deltas.hypothesis: Integer constant width tracks the data type: I8/U8 = 1 raw byte, I32 = 4-byte big-endian, I64 = 8-byte big-endian. Signedness is carried by the type, not the literal bytes (I8 and U8 of 42 both store 0x2a).
deltas.evidence: Numeric42_I8.vi and Numeric42_U8.vi OF__ConstValue = 2a (1 byte, =42); Numeric65536_I64.vi = 00 00 00 00 00 01 00 00 (8 bytes, =65536). Pinned by TestHeapConstValueGroundTruth.

deltas.hypothesis: Floating-point constants store big-endian IEEE-754 of the type's width: SGL = 4-byte binary32, DBL = 8-byte binary64, EXT = 16-byte binary128 (quad, not 80-bit x87). FXD is an 8-byte big-endian fixed-point container (value x 2^k).
deltas.evidence: SGL 46 1a 52 2c = float32(9876.5432)=9876.54297; DBL 40 c3 4a 45 87 93 dd 98 = 9876.5432; EXT 40 0c 34 a4 58 00... = binary128 with unbiased exponent 13 (9876.54297); FXD 00 00 26 94 8b 00 00 00 = 0x26948b=2528395=round(9876.5432*2^8). Pinned by TestHeapConstValueFloatGroundTruth and TestHeapConstValueExtendedAndFixedPoint.

deltas.hypothesis: A complex constant's OF__ConstValue is its two IEEE-754 components written real-then-imaginary, each in the underlying float's big-endian width: CSG = 2x binary32 (8 bytes), CDB = 2x binary64 (16 bytes), CEXT = 2x binary128 (32 bytes). This makes width an ambiguous type key past 8 bytes (16 = EXT or CDB; 32 = CEXT or string), confirming the VCTP type is required to disambiguate.
deltas.evidence: CSG 42+0i = 42 28 00 00 | 00 00 00 00 (2x binary32); CDB 42+5i = 40 45 00 00 00 00 00 00 | 40 14 00 00 00 00 00 00 (2x binary64); CEXT 42+0i = 32 bytes, real-half binary128 exponent 5, imaginary half all zero. Pinned by TestHeapConstValueComplex.

deltas.hypothesis: A block-diagram constant's data type is resolved by joining its OF__ConstValue leaf to a VCTP type descriptor: the governing type is the nearest preceding OF__typeDesc (tag 283) leaf within the same enclosing heap object, whose content is a 0-based index into the VCTP top-types list (NOT a flat VCTP index). TopTypes[content] is the flat VCTP index. A diagram constant carries two typeDesc references (the wired indicator's type and the constant's own); the constant's is the inner, closer one. This join disambiguates the width collisions past 8 bytes (16 = EXT vs CDB, 32 = CEXT vs string) and decodes EXT/CDB/CEXT to exact typed values.
deltas.evidence: Numeric42.vi (I32 const wired to a DBL indicator): OF__typeDesc=0x03 -> TopTypes[3]=3 -> VCTP[3]=NumFloat64 (indicator) and 0x04 -> TopTypes[4]=4 -> VCTP[4]=NumInt32 (constant); nearest-preceding selects 0x04, matching the 4-byte literal. The single-constant fixtures only use content 0x03/0x04 where TopTypes is the identity, hiding the indirection. Add17Plus25.vi (two I32 constants, only 5 VCTP descriptors) is the disambiguator: its constants carry OF__typeDesc=0x07/0x09 indexing an 11-entry TopTypes (TopTypes[7]=TopTypes[9]=4 -> VCTP[4]=NumInt32); flat[7]/flat[9] would be out of range. module-timeout--constant.vi confirms a non-identity map (TopTypes[4]=1 -> VCTP[1]). All 71 corpus block-diagram constants resolve via this scheme. Pinned by TestBlockDiagramConstantTypeJoin / TopTypesIndirection / Integers / Floats / Complex / FixedPoint in pkg/lvvi.

deltas.hypothesis: The block-diagram TopTypes type join carries to the front panel: each front-panel control/indicator/tunnel declares its data type with its own OF__typeDesc (tag 283) leaf in the FPHb heap whose content is a 0-based ordinal into the VCTP top-types list (TopTypes[content] = flat VCTP index). A control's DEFAULT value is an OF__DefaultData (tag 571) leaf governed by the same nearest-preceding-typeDesc rule as a block-diagram constant, so the constant decode machinery (resolveConstTypeIndex + fillTypedConst) decodes scalar defaults directly. A control left at its zero default stores no OF__DefaultData (and there is no DFDS block in the corpus); composite cluster defaults resolve a non-fixed-width type and are surfaced raw (WidthMatch=false) rather than decoded.
deltas.evidence: FP type join: Numeric42.vi panel indicator OF__typeDesc content 0x02 -> TopTypes[2]=3 -> VCTP[3]=NumFloat64 (a non-identity hop), independent of its wired I32 diagram constant; _I8/_SGL/_CDB panels -> NumInt8/NumFloat32/NumComplex128. Scalar default: NumericDblInput.vi, a DBL control with its current value committed as the default (Make Current Value Default), stores one 8-byte OF__DefaultData '40 c3 4a 45 87 93 dd 98' whose nearest-preceding typeDesc resolves to NumFloat64 and decodes to exactly 9876.5432 (WidthMatch=true); the same control before committing the default stored zero OF__DefaultData leaves. Corpus-wide: 278 FPHb typeDesc leaves, 0 OF__ConstValue, 0 DFDS blocks, and 6 OF__DefaultData leaves - the 5 in cluster .ctl/ndjson fixtures are composite (response.ctl: version + 'ok'; error-response.ctl: 'error') and resolve to Void/Type(0x64)/String placeholders, so they stay WidthMatch=false/Kind=unknown. pkg/lvvi.Model.FrontPanelTypes joins each object to its type; FrontPanelDefaults decodes scalar defaults and surfaces composites raw. Pinned by TestFrontPanelTypeJoin / TopTypesIndirection / DefaultScalar / DefaultComposite / NoPanel in pkg/lvvi.
